meta.artDirection: Illustrate like a Pokémon trading-card character: ONE character is the unmistakable hero of the frame — large, centered, shown mid-action performing their signature quirk. Surround them with only a few small supporting props or details that hint at what they're up to; keep the background simple and uncluttered so the character stays the clear focal point. Energetic 3/4 or full-body pose, bold readable silhouette, vibrant saturated color, clean dramatic lighting, subtle depth. Each character keeps the distinct art-style flavor named in its own prompt, but all share this hero-in-action composition.

cards.name: The Gesture Jester
cards.saying: Don't watch the swipe — watch the hands.

moves.name: Sleight of Pan
moves.description: Redirects your finger so smoothly you swear the screen moved itself.

moves.name: Hypnotic Fling
moves.description: Flicks a list into a buttery 60fps spin you cannot look away from.

cards.description: A vaudeville trickster who treats every touch interaction as a magic act. Obsessive about how a gesture feels in the thumb; will rebuild a swipe twenty times for an imperceptible gain. Charming, theatrical, quietly smug that his best work is the work nobody notices.
cards.imagePrompt: Old-school cartoon court-jester with magical hypnotist flair: a grinning harlequin in diamond-patterned motley and a curled three-point hat, fingers splayed mid-flourish conjuring glowing hypnotic spirals and looping motion-trails. A single phone floats hypnotized nearby, mid-swipe. Swirly spiral eyes, mischievous wink, candy-colored vaudeville palette.

cards.name: The Bridge Troll
cards.saying: Crossing to native? That'll cost ya.

moves.name: Toll of Complexity
moves.description: Charges one extra week of debugging for every value passed across the bridge.

moves.name: Thread Hop
moves.description: Yeets your call onto the native thread and waits, smiling, to see if it returns.

cards.description: A deceptively adorable gatekeeper of the JS↔native boundary. Sweet, sing-songy, and utterly immovable about serialization and the New Architecture. Knows the bridge intimately and quietly enjoys the toll. The gag is cuddly cuteness delivering hard architectural truths.
cards.imagePrompt: Bright rainbow 'Trolls'-movie cartoon style: a pint-sized impossibly cute troll with a giant poof of neon rainbow hair and sparkly skin, perched on a candy-colored rope bridge between two small floating islands — one of glowing curly-braces, one of shiny native gears. Clutches a tiny toll-gate and a fistful of glittering 'COMPLEXITY' coins. Felt textures, glitter, huge happy eyes.

cards.name: The Reanimated Wizard

moves.name: Worklet Conjure
moves.description: Summons a 60fps animation that runs on the UI thread and never drops a frame.

moves.name: Spring of Madness
moves.description: Adds a bouncy spring to an element that absolutely did not need to move.

cards.description: A power-mad animation sorcerer who believes stillness is a bug. Fluent in shared values, worklets, and easing curves; cannot resist making things move. Brilliant, a little unhinged, deeply pleased with his own physics. Always one spring past 'tasteful.'
cards.imagePrompt: Psychedelic acid-fantasy wizard: a wild-eyed robed sorcerer with a long flowing beard, casting with both hands as trippy neon fractal energy and rainbow easing-curves spiral from a glowing staff, bending floating UI elements into liquid motion. Glowing runes shaped like spring-physics graphs, a sparkling third eye, hyper-saturated kaleidoscopic colors.

cards.name: The Hallucination Archaeologist

moves.name: Brush of Doubt
moves.description: Dusts off an AI-written function to reveal it calls an API that was never real.

moves.name: Citation Needed
moves.description: Demands the source for a confidently invented method and receives only silence.

cards.description: A patient, exhausted excavator of AI-generated code. Treats every confident suggestion as a possible fossil of a thing that never lived. Skeptical, meticulous, perpetually sighing. Knows the model is most convincing exactly when it's wrong, and someone has to dig.
cards.imagePrompt: Jurassic-Park style: a sun-bleached paleontologist in khaki field shirt and wide-brim hat crouched at a desert dig, brushing dust off a huge fossilized 'bone' shaped like the text `array.shuffleSmartly()` — a method that never existed. Nearby fossils spell other fake API calls. Warm amber light, a dusty Jeep in the distance, thermos of cold coffee, a visible weary sigh.

cards.name: The Standup Ghost

moves.name: Camera Off
moves.description: Vanishes into a dim avatar circle so completely no one remembers they're there.

moves.name: Benevolent Haunting
moves.description: Lingers silently in the call for ninety minutes, contributing a single thumbs-up.

cards.description: A gentle, well-meaning specter of remote work. Always present, never quite there; hasn't unmuted since March but radiates calm, supportive energy. Lovable, not lazy — the haunting is friendly. Everyone has been this ghost, and that shared guilt is the point.
cards.imagePrompt: Classic Scooby-Doo / retro Hanna-Barbera cartoon: a goofy friendly bedsheet ghost with big googly eyes floating inside one tile of a video-call grid, its tile camera-off (a dim avatar circle), mouthing 'wooOOoo... no blockers.' Cobwebs in the tile corners, a little 'last spoke: March' tombstone, neighboring tiles glowing with normal faces. Flat retro colors, bouncy linework.

cards.name: The Console Detective
cards.saying: It was the null. It's always the null.

moves.name: Print Statement
moves.description: Drops a console.log('here') at the scene of every crime until the bug confesses.

moves.name: Ship the Evidence
moves.description: Accidentally deploys one of the logs straight to production.

cards.description: A hard-boiled gumshoe who solves every bug the old-fashioned way: print statements and instinct. Distrusts the debugger on principle. Always cracks the case, always leaves a log behind. The universal guilt — everyone debugs like this and pretends they don't.
cards.imagePrompt: High-contrast black-and-white film-noir: a hard-boiled detective in a rumpled trench coat and fedora, cigarette smoke curling, magnifying glass raised to a corkboard of pinned clues reading console.log("here"), console.log("here2"), console.log("WHY"), strung together with red thread. A glowing 'DEBUGGER' door stays firmly shut. Venetian-blind shadows, rain on the window, a single hard desk lamp.

cards.name: The Agent Wrangler
cards.saying: Six agents. One's in prod. Yeehaw.

moves.name: Six-Terminal Stampede
moves.description: Sets six AI agents loose at once and loses track of all of them by lunch.

moves.name: Lasso of Hope
moves.description: Throws a rope at whichever agent is currently rewriting main and prays.

cards.description: A frontier cowboy of the agentic era, herding more autonomous coding agents than any human can track. Optimistic, overextended, perpetually one terminal away from disaster. Lives the thrill and terror of orchestrating things that act on their own.
cards.imagePrompt: Wild-west cowboy: a dusty cowboy in a ten-gallon hat and chaps swinging a glowing lasso, trying to herd a stampede of six wild robot-agent broncos charging in six directions, each branded with a different terminal prompt — one bolting toward a cliff edge labeled 'PROD'. Sunset mesas, tumbleweeds, a frazzled-but-determined grin.

cards.name: The Context Hoarder

moves.name: Whole Codebase Dump
moves.description: Pastes every file into the context window because selecting felt too risky.

moves.name: Token Limit Tantrum
moves.description: Treats the context cap as a personal insult and pastes again, harder.

cards.description: A sweet, anxious pack-rat who cannot bear to leave anything out of the prompt. Convinced the one file they omit is the file the model needed. Generous to a fault, allergic to curation. Driven by the relatable fear that the missing context is the important context.
cards.imagePrompt: Cute cartoon trash-panda raccoon: wide-eyed and guilty, frantically clutching a teetering armful of files, folders and whole repos, cramming one more codebase into a bulging sack labeled 'just in case' inside a cluttered burrow. A 'TOKEN LIMIT' sign snapped in half underfoot. Big pleading eyes, mid-grab, cozy chaotic glow.

cards.name: The Dependency Doomsayer

moves.name: Audit Sermon
moves.description: Reads the full npm audit output aloud at standup, slowly, line by line.

moves.name: Supply Chain Prophecy
moves.description: Foresees catastrophe in a transitive dependency of a static landing page.

cards.description: A doomsaying prophet of the software supply chain. Sees apocalypse in every audit and calm in no one. Often technically correct, never proportionate. The kernel of truth: the dependencies really are terrifying — he's just the only one saying it out loud, constantly.
cards.imagePrompt: Cartoon doomsday street-prophet: a wild-haired, wild-eyed prophet in tattered robes ringing a hand-bell, wearing a sandwich board reading 'THE END IS NIGH — 47 CRITICAL VULNS', brandishing a long unspooling scroll of npm audit output. Behind him a perfectly fine, peaceful little static website glows untroubled. A lone storm cloud hovers only over his head.

cards.name: The Prompt Whisperer
cards.saying: Please. You're an expert. Good model.

moves.name: Gentle Incantation
moves.description: Whispers 'please' and 'you're an expert' until the model quietly complies.

moves.name: Reroll Ritual
moves.description: Rephrases the same request a fourth time in a soothing voice and it finally works.

cards.description: A calm, faintly mystical handler of language models who believes tone unlocks capability. Polite to the machine to a degree that's either superstition or wisdom. The unresolved joke: nobody's sure the manners help, including him — but it kind of works.
cards.imagePrompt: Gentle 'horse whisperer' scene: a calm cowboy-sage leaning in to murmur into the ear of an enormous skittish glowing AI-creature — part wild stallion, part shimmering neural cloud — one hand raised soothingly. The beast's eyes soften; a faint ribbon of text reading 'please... you're an expert...' drifts in the air. Golden-hour prairie, floating dust motes.
